# Laundromat Industry Snapshot — TheDealSheet

**Author:** TheDealSheet Editorial
**Source:** [thedealsheet.co](https://thedealsheet.co/industries/laundromat)
**Saved:** 2026-04-20

## Why I Saved This

G&B considered and REJECTED laundromat. Kay wants TDS's PE-POV cross-reference to confirm or challenge the rejection. Snapshot preserved for future reference in case sub-niche edges or ecosystem-adjacent plays (luxury linen / hospitality laundry / specialty textile cleaning) surface downstream. Does not reopen laundromat or commercial laundry equipment service (commercial laundry equipment service was PASSED 4/19 — Alliance/EVI consolidating dealer channel).

## Content

### Market Size & Growth
- U.S. market: $6.8B (2025, IBISWorld)
- CAGR: 1.6% through 2026, flat to negative through 2030
- Business count: 18,375–30,000 locations; -1.5% CAGR 2021–2026
- Revenue mix: Self-service 70% / Wash-Dry-Fold + On-Demand 25% / Ancillary 5%
- Fastest-growing segment: Wash-dry-fold/pickup-delivery at 12.5% CAGR

### Fragmentation
Extreme. 17,461 businesses. PE accounts for only 5–15% of deal volume. Top operators (CSC ServiceWorks, SuperSuds, Laundry Growth Partners) are early-stage regional consolidators — not national platforms.

### Margins (unit economics)
| Metric | Industry | Top quartile |
|---|---|---|
| EBITDA margin | 28–35% | 38–45% |
| Median SDE | $68K–$85K | — |
| Utilities % revenue | 15–20% | <15% w/ sub-metering |

Gross margins by service: Self-service 65–75% / Wash-Dry-Fold 40–50% / Pickup-Delivery 45–55% / Specialty (pet/hypo) 50–60%.

### Valuation Multiples
| Revenue Band | Multiple | Metric |
|---|---|---|
| $500K–$750K | 2.7–3.2x | SDE |
| $750K–$1.2M | 3.2–3.8x | SDE |
| $1.2M–$2M | 3.8–4.2x | SDE |
| $2M+ multi-unit | 4.0–4.5x | SDE→EBITDA |

Premium drivers: 30%+ wash-fold mix, card/mobile payments, 8+ yr assignable lease, <12yr machines, high-density location, semi-absentee w/ SOPs.
Discount drivers: coin-only ("ZombieMat"), lease <24mo, deferred maintenance, utilities >20%.

### Recurring Revenue
90% customer repeat rate, 94.8% five-year survival, but no contracted recurring revenue. Consumer walk-in, non-subscription (except emerging Cleanly/app-based wash-fold subscriptions, small share).

### PE Activity (Named Platforms)
- **CSC ServiceWorks** — PE-backed, largest independent, continuous add-on
- **SuperSuds** — Bob Schwartz search-fund exit, 5-store platform, DE/MD/PA/VA
- **Laundry Growth Partners** — Emerging PE, undisclosed sponsor
- **Cents** — SaaS platform, $40M Series B (1517 Fund), 2,700+ locations (software, not operator roll-up)
- **EVI Industries** — Acquired Girbau North America for $43M April 2025 (upstream equipment M&A)

### M&A Pace
~100 deals/yr 2022–2023, 138 deals 2024 (+32% YoY), 200–400/yr 2025 run-rate. PE add-ons +5–10%.

### Median Deal Terms
Median sale price $275K. SBA 7(a) financing typical at 8.5%/10yr.

### Owner Demographics / Deal Flow
Primarily owner-operators; semi-absentee with attendants; 35% labor turnover; single-location dominant; seller aging cohort driving listings.

### Regulatory (material)
- **EPA Water Efficiency** — 40% water reduction mandate June 2026 for commercial washers
- **California SB 707** — Textile EPR July 2026
- **Digital payment mandates** — Rolling across jurisdictions
- **OSHA 1910.264** — Machine guarding
- **ADA** — $15–30K initial accessibility capex
- **Lease assignment** — Kills 25–30% of deals; single biggest transaction risk

### Operational Challenges
- Utility cost volatility (15–20% of revenue, 3–6 month pass-through lag)
- Lease assignment failure (25–30% of deals die here)
- Equipment obsolescence ($5–10K per machine replacement; $150–300K full refresh)
- Tech capex for card/mobile conversion ($15–30K)
- PE scaling limits — margins don't meaningfully improve at 50 locations vs 2 (WSO PE commentary cited)

### Geographic Sweet Spots
Austin, Nashville, Denver (top 3). Caution: SF (water costs), Detroit, Cleveland. California has SB 707 + water costs = structural headwind.

### TDS Final Take (their verdict)
"Boring is beautiful" for operator-searchers, not institutional PE. Sweet spot = $750K–$1.5M revenue, card-enabled, 30%+ wash-fold, long-term assignable lease, Sun Belt metro. Buy at 3.2–3.8x SDE, SBA finance, drive 20–30% revenue growth via tech + premium services, hold 5–7 years for 25% cash-on-cash. Exit at 4.0–4.5x for multi-unit platforms.

## Key Patterns

- **Pattern 1:** TDS explicitly flags "PE scaling limitations — margins don't improve materially at 50 locations vs 2" — confirms laundromat fails the platform thesis G&B requires. Location economics dominate portfolio economics.
- **Pattern 2:** Consumer-retail customer base (renters, 67% of urban housing) — direct contradiction of G&B's luxury/high-value-asset thesis. No luxury customer proxy in core business.
- **Pattern 3:** Lease is "most valuable asset AND highest risk" — structural fragility inappropriate for a family holdco targeting 25+ year holds. Every acquisition is a new real-estate diligence cycle.
- **Pattern 4:** No contracted recurring revenue. 90% repeat rate is behavioral not contractual — can't underwrite to it in an LOI.
- **Pattern 5:** Named PE platforms are small regional consolidators (SuperSuds = search fund, Laundry Growth = undisclosed). This is not a mature PE category — it's a searcher category. G&B would be competing with search-fund principals on their sweet-spot assets.
- **Pattern 6:** Regulatory headwind stack (EPA water, CA SB 707, ADA, lease assignment, digital payment mandates) all compound over time. Compliance drag rather than moat.
- **Pattern 7:** 28–35% EBITDA margins are real, but sit on a 1.6% growth industry declining to flat/negative post-2030. Buying operator cash flow in a shrinking category.

## How I Might Use This

- **Confirms rejection** — TDS data validates G&B's laundromat pass on thesis, not just execution. File as closed.
- **Sub-niche watchpoint** — Hospitality linen service, luxury dry cleaning / textile restoration for fine garments and art textiles might fit G&B (B2B, luxury-customer-adjacent, recurring contracts), but these are adjacent industries not sub-niches of laundromat. Flag for future niche-intelligence pass; do NOT re-propose commercial laundry equipment services (PASSED 4/19).
- **Searcher-competition signal** — Reinforces `feedback_searcher_overlap`: laundromat is core search-fund territory, not G&B layer.
- **PE scaling failure pattern** — "Margins don't improve at scale" is a useful filter for other location-based services (car washes, self-storage-adjacent) — add to niche screening heuristics.
